Effective governance of the Agriculture Sector
Legal, policy, regulations and standards, guidelines and protocols development and reviews
Development of the livestock value chain
Ruminant (cattle, sheep & goats) production and marketing
Poultry, pig, and micro livestock production
Animal health and livestock diseases management
Enhancement of food production and productivity
Crop value chains and food systems promotion (food and cash crops of state’s comparative advantage)
Intensive crop and vegetable production (irrigation, crop diversification etc.)
Farm inputs supply and service delivery system (improved seeds, fertilizer, agro-chemicals etc.)
Modern technology for post-harvest storage and value addition
Enhancement of fisheries resources development (aquaculture, marine, inland, artisanal)
Commercial aquaculture development (fish production, feed mills development, fishing inputs etc.)
Promotion of forest resource conservation and preservation of biodiversity
Non-farm forestry livelihood economics empowerment promotion (apiculture, sericulture etc.) programme
Promotion of enabling environment for increased agricultural development
Integrated rural development (agricultural land development, farm mechanization, & rural infrastructures
Youth and women In agriculture empowerment & smallholder agricultural credit strengthening
Agricultural data and statistic management and institutionalisation of information and communication technology (ICT)
Adaptive research, unified and all-inclusive extension services delivery
Public Private Partnerships (corporate private sector, NGOs, donors & development partners, farmers organizations)
Capacity building for stakeholders and professional human resources development
Agriculture Sector Expenditures Not Elsewhere Classified
Effective governance of the health system
Human and institutional capacity performance management
Community engagement and participation in health
Enhancement of the delivery of Essential Package of Health Services (EPHS) to all citizens
Provision of the right number and right skill mix of competent, motivated, and productive Human Resources for Health (HRH)
Provision of adequate and modern health infrastructure for health services delivery
Provision of quality, affordable, available, and safe medicines, vaccines, and other health commodities
Research and development (Institutional Review Board, Clinical Trials)
Institution and maintenance of a responsive public health emergency preparedness system
Provision of universal health coverage and financial risk protection for citizens
Mobilising equity contributions and vulnerable group funds
Mobilising employers’ contributions to the State Social Health Insurance Scheme
Health Sector Expenditures Not Elsewhere Classified
Effective governance of the education system
Increase in access, retention, and completion rate at all levels
Early Childhood Care, Development and Education (ECCDE)
Equity and inclusiveness in the provision of educational services
Improved quality of teaching and learning outcomes
All levels of education quality assurance
Teaching and non-teaching staff capacity building
